model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Kansas is a top-25 team in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite defense ranked 10th in adjusted defensive efficiency, while California Baptist lacks any available advanced metrics, indicating they are a low-major automatic qualifier with a significant talent gap. The Jayhawks' experience in the Big 12 and proven ability to dominate defensively make them the overwhelming favorite, and the underdog's only path would require a perfect shooting night and Kansas having an off game, which is highly unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate Team Quality and Availability
reasoningStep.summary: I checked Kansas's advanced ratings and searched for California Baptist's season context, finding Kansas is a top-25 team with elite defense and no injury concerns, while California Baptist has no available metrics, suggesting a major underdog.
reasoningStep.evidence: Kansas ranks 21st in KenPom and 22nd in Torvik with a 23-10 record and a top-10 defense; California Baptist has null ratings in both systems, indicating they are a low-major champion with no comparable data.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
